title: refactor(poster): consolidate comment parsing into single preprocessing step
body:
## Summary

Currently `analyzeFindingStatuses` and `filterDuplicateFindings` both iterate over comments and extract fingerprints independently. This causes redundant parsing and creates two sources of truth that could drift.

## Current Behavior

1. `analyzeFindingStatuses()` iterates comments → builds `map[fingerprint]status`
2. `filterDuplicateFindings()` iterates comments again → builds `map[fingerprint]bool` via `extractBotFingerprints()`

Both functions parse the same comment set but derive fingerprints separately.

## Proposed Solution

Consolidate into a single preprocessing step that returns a structured view:

```go
type CommentAnalysis struct {
    Fingerprints map[domain.FindingFingerprint]bool
    Statuses     map[domain.FindingFingerprint]domain.FindingStatus
    StatusCounts StatusCounts
    // Could also include thread info, reply metadata, etc.
}

func analyzeComments(comments []github.PullRequestComment, botUsername string) CommentAnalysis
```

Then all dedup stages (Stage 0, Stage 1, Stage 2) operate on this shared structure.

## Benefits

- Single iteration over comments
- Single source of truth for fingerprint extraction
- No risk of drift if parsing rules change
- Easier to extend with additional analysis

## Files

- `internal/usecase/github/poster.go` - Main refactoring target

## Context

Identified during PR #255 code review by architecture and performance reviewers.
